faqs.question: Why do I have crickets in my Clifton home?
faqs.answer: Crickets are attracted to Clifton homes seeking moisture, warmth, and shelter, especially in basements, crawl spaces, and areas near outdoor lighting. Staten Island's climate and the abundance of vegetation in residential areas make cricket activity common, particularly in late summer and fall.
